bids.reasoning: It is the very beginning of the negotiation (t=0.0) and the opponent has opened with an offer that yields my lowest possible utility on all issues. I will counter by asserting my ideal preferences to establish a strong starting position, proposing the maximum utility for myself across the board.

The negotiation has just started (t=0.02), and the opponent has repeated their opening offer which provides me with minimum utility (0.20). I will show a slight willingness to compromise by conceding marginally on my least important issue (issueD) while maintaining my most preferred values for all other issues, aiming for an initial high utility of 0.973.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening offer of all 'valueD'. Because it is very early in the negotiation (t=0.04), I will continue to hold firm on my highest-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) while making a minor concession on my lowest-weight issue (issueD) to signal a willingness to move.

The negotiation is still in the very early stages (t=0.06). The opponent has rigidly repeated their opening offer which serves only their utility and provides me with the lowest possible outcome. Since I still have ample time, I will maintain my positions on my highest-weight issues (issueA and issueB) to protect my core interests, while presenting a minor concession on my lower-weight issues (issueC and issueD) to encourage reciprocal movement.

The opponent has made a significant concession on issueB and issueC (offering valueA for both), while holding firm on issueA (valueD) and conceding slightly on issueD (valueC). Because the negotiation is still very early (t=0.08) and issueA is my most heavily weighted issue (0.4), I will hold firm on issueA. I will match their values on issueB and issueC, and fully concede issueD to valueD, as issueD is my lowest-weighted issue.

The current time is very early (t=0.1). The opponent has explored a few different combinations, recently offering valueA on issueB and issueC before retreating to low-utility bids. Because issueA and issueB are my most important issues, I will hold firm on valueA for both, while making small concessions on my lower-weighted issues (issueC and issueD) to find common ground without sacrificing too much utility. This bid yields a strong utility of ~0.89 while offering a new valid variation.

It is still early in the negotiation (t=0.12), so I am maintaining a high target utility for my offers. The opponent showed temporary interest in valueA for issueB and issueC at t=0.08, but has since reverted to less favorable offers. Given my high weight for issueA, I will hold firm on valueA there, keep valueA for issueB, and concede marginally on issueC and issueD to gauge if this combination appeals to them without sacrificing too much utility on my end.

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.14), so I want to maintain a high utility target while making a small concession to signal cooperation. I notice the opponent briefly offered value A for issues B and C previously, but reverted to their initial values when I didn't reach an agreement on issue A. I will hold firm on issue A since it is my highest priority, but make my first concession on issue B to see if they are responsive to variations there, while keeping utility around 0.86.

The current time fraction is 0.16, which is still very early in the negotiation. The opponent is stubbornly repeating their low-utility offer (for us) consisting mostly of 'valueD' and 'valueC'. I am slowly exploring concessions on the lower-weighted issues (issueC and issueD) while holding firm on the most important issues to encourage the opponent to concede.

The opponent just proposed an offer (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueD) that yields a high utility of 0.866 for me. Since this perfectly matches one of my earlier offers and grants me my top preferences on the most critical issues (A and B), I will accept it by mirroring the bid exactly. This secures an excellent outcome early in the negotiation.
